writePrerequisites.description: pact-validate must consume the persisted write snapshot from disk before deciding whether provider verification is honestly ready, partial, blocked, irrelevant, or inconsistent.

planSupportInputs.description: pact-validate must read the persisted plan snapshot from disk as the canonical prior verdict and scaffold budget for validation.

scanSupportInputs.description: pact-validate must read the persisted scan snapshot from disk as supporting evidence for provider identity, artifact source expectations, and provider-state readiness.

requiredMachineState.description: Canonical persisted validation result for Pact provider verification. It must capture the validated input verdict chain, repo reality checks, compile/structural proof, runnable verification proof, remaining blockers, and honest notes without pretending readiness unsupported by persisted state and repo evidence.

validationOutcomeTaxonomy.description: pact-validate must distinguish truly ready repos from partial, blocked, irrelevant, and inconsistent/drifted states.

stopConditions.description: One or more required persisted scan/plan/write inputs are missing, so validation cannot proceed honestly.
Persisted scan/plan/write verdicts conflict with one another, so the validation chain is inconsistent.
Files claimed as written or modified no longer match the recorded Pact scaffold outcome.
Repo reality may be structurally sound, but runnable Pact verification remains blocked or unproven by the available evidence.
Persisted scan/plan/write state honestly shows Pact provider verification is irrelevant for this repo within the current MVP boundary.

summaryState.description: Human-readable Pact validate summary only. Canonical machine state stays in validate-state.json.
